patches.rationale: ISS-1219: remove ;return false from OTS canary anchor in SEC-5.0 to allow anchor jump to complete after tab switch. Matches canonical pattern in WIN-058/058b/062.

patches.find: That mechanism — multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics, and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind.
patches.replace: That mechanism — potential-field continuation of the deep-source geodynamo signal upward through mantle and crust, modulated by depth-dependent crustal susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics (IGRF / WMM-class models), and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind.
patches.rationale: ISS-1305: "magnetic conduction" is physically incorrect; the mechanism is potential-field continuation (Laplace equation / spherical harmonic upward continuation). Susceptibility modulates the crustal contribution but is not the primary transport mechanism.

patches.find: The value has been in the scientific record for over a century.
patches.replace: The measurement is over a century old (Tesla 1899) and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest.
patches.rationale: ISS-1247: tighten with dated anchors (1899 measurement, 1978 Beograd publication) to close the "what counts as scientific record" pivot. Both dates already appear in adjacent sentences.

patches.find: The mechanism is also wrong. The dome's confirming text invokes the agonic lines (longitudes where magnetic declination = 0). Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic currently runs near 95–100°W (through the central United States), 25–30° west of the Bermuda Triangle's eastern boundary at ~65°W. The eastern agonic at ~100–120°E lies 17–37° west of the Devil's Sea (137°E). Neither agonic line passes through its associated region. This is independent of the symmetry-tolerance debate: even granting "near-antipodal" loosely, the supposed mechanism (agonic-line correlation) does not hold quantitatively in either direction. The dome's claimed pattern does not correspond to any feature in IGRF or CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field models.
patches.replace: The mechanism (steelman test). The dome publishes no mechanism for the claimed symmetry. We test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at roughly the same latitude across hemispheres — agonic-line correlation — as our charitable steelman. Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic currently runs near 95–100°W (through the central United States), 25–30° west of the Bermuda Triangle's eastern boundary at ~65°W. The eastern agonic at ~100–120°E lies 17–37° west of the Devil's Sea (137°E). Neither agonic line passes through its associated region. This is independent of the symmetry-tolerance debate: even granting "near-antipodal" loosely, the supposed mechanism (agonic-line correlation) does not hold quantitatively in either direction. The dome's claimed pattern does not correspond to any feature in IGRF or CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field models. If the dome wishes to propose a different mechanism, the burden is to publish one; without a published mechanism the claim is additionally unfalsifiable on mechanistic grounds.
patches.rationale: WIN-028 c6 H0 (major): strawman risk — dome publishes no mechanism, but our text attributes agonic-line invocation. Reframe with dual-reading frame: "no mechanism published; we steelman agonic-line correlation." Geographic content unchanged; adds unfalsifiability-on-mechanism note.

patches.find: The 28° gap is a 15.6% deviation. By the dome's own confirmation thresholds elsewhere in V51.1, that is a wide miss. WIN-029 takes a 5.3% agreement between the dome's quarter-wave Schumann calculation and its model height as confirmatory ("H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model"). WIN-027 reports R² = 0.7874 for a fitted polynomial as confirmation. WIN-030 cites a 48.6% RMS reduction as a positive confirmation. A 15.6% off-target is roughly three times the residual the dome treats as a CONFIRMED match in WIN-029, and on the same order as the ratio errors the dome itself flagged as failures in earlier model versions ("v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances"). It is not a confirmatory match in the dome's own framework.
patches.replace: The 28° gap is a 15.6% deviation (relative to the target of 180° — the only standard mathematical referent for “geometric symmetry” between two surface points; deviation as fraction of target is the natural relative-error metric). By the dome's own confirmation thresholds elsewhere in V51.1, that is a wide miss. WIN-029 takes a 5.3% agreement between the dome's quarter-wave Schumann calculation and its model height as confirmatory — we do not endorse 5.3% as a meaningful tolerance (WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review), but cite it here to show the dome's own framework treats single-digit residuals as the bar. WIN-027 reports R² = 0.7874 for a fitted polynomial as confirmation; WIN-030 cites a 48.6% RMS reduction (these are distinct statistical measures from a residual percentage, but each reflects the dome accepting tight fits as its standard). A 15.6% residual is roughly three times the WIN-029 5.3% residual benchmark. It is not a confirmatory match in the dome's own framework.
patches.rationale: WIN-028 c6 H1-H4 consolidated: (H1) add denominator justification for 15.6%; (H2) add internal-consistency parenthetical on WIN-029 being Self-Contradicted; (H3) clarify R²/RMS are distinct statistical measures from residual percentage; (H4) remove v22 cite (v22 failure was 100% deviation, not comparable to 15%).
